summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, CS education / AI policy, Democracy / anti-fascism. The strongest items in this run are Oil price soars to highest level since 2022 as US-Iran impasse shows no sign of resolution, Brown University shooting suspect driven by ‘accumulation of grievances’, FBI says, and ‘Your questions are designed to trick me’: combative Musk grilled over battle with Sam Altman.

mainItems.title: Oil price soars to highest level since 2022 as US-Iran impasse shows no sign of resolution
mainItems.summary: Oil markets spooked as Donald Trump appears willing to maintain the US Navy blockade and Iran keeps strait of Hormuz all but shut The price of Brent oil soared above $122 a barrel on Wednesday, its highest level since 2022, as US-Iran…

mainItems.title: Brown University shooting suspect driven by ‘accumulation of grievances’, FBI says
mainItems.summary: Claudio Neves Valente, who killed himself after deadly attack, began planning for violence in 2022, authorities say The gunman behind a deadly shooting at Brown University in December appeared to have been aggrieved by personal failures…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: ‘Your questions are designed to trick me’: combative Musk grilled over battle with Sam Altman
mainItems.summary: Lawyers for world’s richest person try to paint him as humanitarian as judge cuts off his long-winded replies After a dramatic first day of opening statements and testimony from Elon Musk in his case against Sam Altman and OpenAI, the…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Trump hosts crew of historic Artemis mission: ‘I would have had no trouble making it’
mainItems.summary: The astronauts joined president in Oval Office for a press conference, and it wasn’t long before he praised himself Donald Trump hosted the crew of the historic Artemis II lunar flyby mission at the White House on Wednesday.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to state power, civil liberties, and anti-authoritarian concerns.

mainItems.title: Families sue OpenAI over Canadian mass shooter's use of ChatGPT
mainItems.summary: The lawsuits claim OpenAI was negligent for failing to report the shooter to authorities after her account was flagged for "gun violence activity and planning."
mainItems.whyItMatters: Touches trust, surveillance, and the private use of AI systems.

mainItems.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
mainItems.summary: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: Elon Musk accuses OpenAI's leaders of 'looting the nonprofit' in court testimony
mainItems.summary: In his second day on the stand in the trial he launched against OpenAI, Elon Musk said the AI start-up he'd helped found had strayed from its charitable mission.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: "Those of us who joined in the early 2000s, we had it very good": Monument Valley boss says it's getting harder to give workers full-time stability
mainItems.summary: After Monument Valley 3 was unceremoniously pulled from Netflix last year, series developers Ustwo Games are now focusing on PC first and foremost.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Welcome to 'Anxietyland' theme park, where the rides are no fun
mainItems.summary: From the Emotional Roller Coaster to the Worry-go-round, cartoonist Gemma Correll walks us through her brain's not-so-amusing amusement park in a darkly funny memoir .
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to local models, open tooling, and hands-on experimentation.

mainItems.title: A look at one of the farms RFK Jr. has pointed to as a model for 'reparenting'
mainItems.summary: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.

mainItems.summary: It turns out that big Daniel scene wasn't in the episode's original script The post Daniel’s Story in Daredevil: Born Again Episode 7 Was Changed in Post-Production appeared first on Reactor .

youtube.title: Finding Hardware Bugs - Computerphile
youtube.summary: When you're setting your hardware design out using automated tools is essential, but what if the tools themselves have bugs in them? John P Wickerson is based at Imperial College London.
youtube.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

trailers.title: Worst Ex Ever Season 2 | Official Trailer
trailers.summary: Are they "the one" or just the one you were warned about? Worst Ex Ever returns to Netflix on May 6 for Season 2 with true, harrowing tales of monstrous relationships and unhinged exes.
trailers.whyItMatters: Potentially worth attention if the trailer looks more interesting than generic studio sludge.

trailers.title: Insidious: Out Of The Further | Official Trailer
trailers.summary: The Further comes for our world.
trailers.whyItMatters: Potentially worth attention if the trailer looks more interesting than generic studio sludge.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

new.title: Constellation
new.why: A little more cerebral and mood-heavy, but it scratches the identity / reality-slippage itch.

new.title: The Devil’s Hour
new.why: Dark, twisty, and structurally ambitious without feeling like homework.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

older.title: Continuum
older.why: A cleaner old-school sci-fi fit if you want procedural momentum wrapped around time-travel politics.

older.title: Person of Interest
older.why: Starts procedural and then quietly becomes one of the smartest AI / surveillance shows on television.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: Solve a murder mystery by flying people around on your giant crane in the Disco Elysium and Chinese folktale inspired The Crane Rider's Tale
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing at James's request.

highlights.title: 12 unexpected sourdough bread recipes that aren't crusty loaves: From sourdough flatbread to sourdough milk bread — and even sourdough babka.
highlights.notes: Pinned from today's briefing, the kind of bread-nerd rabbit hole worth keeping.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
